publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

did.physdesc: Correspondence, photographs, publication reprints, patient record notes, schoolwork, and manuscripts.
did.abstract: Includes correspondence, photographs, publication reprints, patient record notes, schoolwork, and manuscripts. Materials relating to her service in WWI, such as her application, contract, and petitions for veteran benefits, are also included. Tjomsland's correspondence covers both her daily and professional life, including her work as a translator and writer. Also includes her correspondence with Morris Bishop, author of "A History of Cornell," about her experience as a student in Burt Green Wilder's class.

defitem.label: Contact Information:
defitem.item: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.eduhttp://rmc.library.cornell.edu

bioghist.head: BIOGRAPHICAL NOTE
bioghist.p: Dr. Anne Tjomsland (Cornell University B.A. 1911, M.D. 1914) worked at Bellevue Hospital in New York City before serving as a contract surgeon for the U.S. Army in Vichy, France during World War I. She wrote about her experiences with the Bellevue Hospital medical unit in her book "Bellevue in France: Anecdotal History of Base Hospital No. 1." When she returned to the U.S. Tjomsland continued to practice medicine as a general practitioner and anesthesiologist. She also began pursuing a Ph. D. in history at Columbia University, stopping a few credits short. Tjomsland continued writing, both fiction and non-fiction, as well translating Norwegian medical histories and Icelandic mythology. In 1951, Cornell University Library issued her translation of "The Saga of Hrafn Sveinbjarnarson: The Life of an Icelandic Physician of the Thirteenth Century" as Islandica Volume 35.

scopecontent.head: COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
scopecontent.p: Includes correspondence, photographs, publication reprints, patient record notes, schoolwork, and manuscripts. Materials relating to her service in WWI, such as her application, contract, and petitions for veteran benefits, are also included. Tjomsland's correspondence covers both her daily and professional life, including her work as a translator and writer. Also includes her correspondence with Morris Bishop, author of "A History of Cornell," about her experience as a student in Burt Green Wilder's class.

relatedmaterial.head: RELATED MATERIALS
relatedmaterial.p: Related collections: Mary Merritt Crawford and Family Papers, #41-5-619

arrangement.head: SERIES LIST
arrangement.p: Series II. World War I Related Materials, 1914-1963 Box 3
Series III. Writing and Translations, 1910-1963 Box 3

prefercite.head: Cite As:
prefercite.p: Anne Tjomsland papers, #2924. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

accessrestrict.head: Access Restrictions:
accessrestrict.p: Access restricted to all individually identifiable health information in patients' medical records in Box 4 and Box 5 Folder 8 per the HIPAA Privacy Rule.

did.unittitle: Correspondence on Reichborn-Kjennerud obituary and pamphlets
Ciba - The History of the White Potato, National Potato Council correspondence
History of Medicine by Robin Fahraeus correspondence
D.H. Daugherty, American Council of Learned Societies correspondence
Erik J. Friis, Robert Hunter related correspondence

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains materials related to Tjomsland's service as a contract surgeon in World War I.

did.unittitle: WWI photo prints and Norwegian newsprint
WWI U.S. Army application and related materials
U.S. Treasury Department Patriotic Cooperation citation
Veterans' Exemption from Real Property Taxes application
American Heritage History of WWI book promotion

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains fiction, non-fiction, and translated manuscripts, including her history of the Bellevue Hospital medical unit in France during World War I.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Under the HIPAA Privacy Rule, all individually identifiable health information in patients' medical records Box 5 Folder 8 are restricted.

did.unittitle: The Origins and Growth of Bellevue Hospital

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged chronologically, if no date arranged alphabetically.

did.unittitle: The Captain of the Men of Death
On Disorders of the Pulse Beat
Pastor Karl og Hermana Schoning kom til Sogne. Et barndomsminne.
The Saga of Hrafn Sveinbjarnarson translation
A Shirt for Frederick the Thresher-Man
The Bear Hunters in Bjollelfa translation

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains mainly coursework from Columbia University, as well as work prior to college.

did.unittitle: Columbia University History of Italian Medicine
Columbia University History of Science notebook
Columbia University History 25-26: Intro to the History of Science

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes notes on Vichy, anesthesia, Falkberget, and address books. Some notes are in shorthand.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes obituaries, reprints, and book reviews.

did.unittitle: Application for Public Health Service Research Grant

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged chronologically, if no date arranged alphabetically.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Under the HIPAA Privacy Rule, all individually identifiable health information in patients' medical records in Box 4 are restricted.

did.unittitle: Dinner at 501 and France photographs, assorted painting reprints

10th Congresso Internacional de Historia de la Medicina related materials
